components.name: Carbon Film Resistor
components.description: A passive component that limits or controls the flow of electric current using a thin carbon film deposited on a ceramic core.
components.workingPrinciple: Current flowing through the carbon film encounters resistance proportional to the film's resistivity, length, and cross-sectional area, converting electrical energy into heat (Ohm's Law: V = IR).
components.voltageRating: Depends on power rating, typically up to 250V
components.currentRating: Determined by P = I²R, typically mA range
advantages: Low noise compared to composition resistors
disadvantages: Temperature coefficient higher than metal film
components.typicalUses: General-purpose current limiting and voltage dropping in hobby and consumer electronics.
components.ttsSummary: Carbon film resistor. A general purpose resistor used to limit current.

components.name: Metal Film Resistor
components.description: A precision resistor made by depositing a thin metal alloy film on a ceramic substrate, offering tighter tolerance and lower noise than carbon types.
components.workingPrinciple: Resistance arises from the metal alloy's resistivity; the thin uniform film allows tight control of resistance value during manufacturing (laser trimming).
components.typicalUses: Precision voltage references, audio circuits, instrumentation amplifiers.
components.ttsSummary: Metal film resistor. A precision resistor with tight tolerance.

components.name: Wire Wound Resistor
components.description: A resistor made by winding a resistive metal wire (often nichrome) around a ceramic or fiberglass core, built for high power dissipation.
components.workingPrinciple: Current passes through a long thin resistive wire; resistance depends on wire length, diameter, and resistivity, with the winding allowing compact high-value or high-power resistors.
disadvantages: Higher inductance, less suitable for high frequency
components.typicalUses: Power dissipation in industrial and motor drive applications.
components.ttsSummary: Wire wound resistor. Used for high power applications.

components.name: Variable Resistor (Rheostat)
components.description: An adjustable resistor that allows the user to manually change resistance, typically using a sliding or rotating contact.
components.workingPrinciple: A wiper contact moves along a resistive track, changing the effective length of resistive material in the circuit and thus the resistance.
components.voltageRating: Depends on rating, up to several hundred volts
components.currentRating: Up to several amps for power rheostats
components.typicalUses: Manual adjustment of current/voltage in power and control circuits.
components.ttsSummary: Variable resistor, also called a rheostat.

components.name: Potentiometer
components.description: A 3-terminal variable resistor used to create an adjustable voltage divider.
components.workingPrinciple: A wiper slides along a resistive track between two fixed terminals, tapping off a variable fraction of the applied voltage.
components.voltageRating: Up to 50V typical for signal pots
components.typicalUses: Audio volume knobs, trimming circuits, joystick position sensing.
components.ttsSummary: Potentiometer. A three terminal variable resistor.

components.name: Preset (Trimmer Potentiometer)
components.description: A small potentiometer designed for infrequent adjustment, typically set once during calibration using a screwdriver.
components.workingPrinciple: Same as a potentiometer but designed for board-mounted, screwdriver-adjusted, low-cycle-life applications.
components.typicalUses: One-time calibration of reference voltages or gain.
components.ttsSummary: Preset trimmer potentiometer used for circuit calibration.

components.name: Ceramic Capacitor
components.description: A capacitor using ceramic material as the dielectric, available in small non-polarized packages.
components.workingPrinciple: Two conductive plates separated by a ceramic dielectric store charge; capacitance depends on plate area, separation, and dielectric constant.
components.typicalUses: Decoupling power rails near ICs, high-frequency filtering.
components.ttsSummary: Ceramic capacitor. Commonly used for decoupling and filtering.

components.name: Electrolytic Capacitor
components.description: A polarized capacitor with high capacitance values, using an electrolyte to achieve a larger effective surface area.
components.workingPrinciple: An oxide layer on the anode foil acts as the dielectric, while the liquid/gel electrolyte serves as the cathode, allowing very high capacitance per volume.
components.typicalUses: Smoothing rectified DC, power supply bulk storage.
components.ttsSummary: Electrolytic capacitor. Polarized capacitor used for power filtering.

components.name: Tantalum Capacitor
components.description: A polarized capacitor using tantalum metal for the anode, offering stable capacitance and small size for its value.
components.workingPrinciple: A tantalum pentoxide layer forms the dielectric on a sintered tantalum pellet, with a manganese dioxide or polymer cathode providing high capacitance density.
components.typicalUses: Space-constrained decoupling in portable electronics.
components.ttsSummary: Tantalum capacitor. Compact and stable polarized capacitor.

components.name: Film Capacitor
components.description: A non-polarized capacitor using a thin plastic film as the dielectric, known for stability and low losses.
components.workingPrinciple: Metal foil or metallized film electrodes are separated by a plastic film dielectric (polyester, polypropylene), wound or stacked to form the capacitor.
disadvantages: Larger than ceramic for same capacitance
components.typicalUses: High-fidelity audio coupling, AC filtering, snubber networks.
components.ttsSummary: Film capacitor. Known for stability in audio and timing circuits.

components.name: Mica Capacitor
components.description: A high-precision capacitor using mica sheets as dielectric, valued for stability at radio frequencies.
components.workingPrinciple: Silver electrodes are deposited directly on mica sheets, stacked together to form a stable, low-loss capacitor.
components.typicalUses: RF tuning circuits and crystal oscillator networks.
components.ttsSummary: Mica capacitor. Used in radio frequency tuning circuits.

components.name: Super Capacitor (Ultracapacitor)
components.description: An energy storage device with extremely high capacitance, bridging the gap between capacitors and batteries.
components.workingPrinciple: Energy is stored electrostatically at the interface of activated carbon electrodes and electrolyte (electric double-layer), allowing capacitance in the Farad range.
components.typicalUses: Short-term high-power backup and burst energy delivery.
components.ttsSummary: Super capacitor, also called an ultracapacitor, for high power bursts.

components.name: Toroidal Inductor
components.description: An inductor wound on a donut-shaped (toroidal) magnetic core, offering high efficiency and low electromagnetic interference.
components.workingPrinciple: Current through the coil creates a magnetic field confined within the toroidal core, storing energy and opposing changes in current (Faraday's law).
components.voltageRating: Depends on insulation, typically up to several hundred volts
components.currentRating: Up to tens of amps depending on wire gauge
components.typicalUses: Switch-mode power supply filtering and EMI suppression.
components.ttsSummary: Toroidal inductor wound on a donut shaped magnetic core.

components.name: Axial Inductor
components.description: A cylindrical inductor with leads extending from both ends, similar in shape to an axial resistor.
components.workingPrinciple: A coil of wire wound around a ferrite or air core stores energy in a magnetic field, opposing rapid changes in current.
advantages: Easy to mount like a resistor
components.typicalUses: RF and signal line filtering chokes.
components.ttsSummary: Axial inductor used for RF and signal filtering.

components.name: SMD Inductor
components.description: A surface-mount inductor in a compact chip package for space-constrained PCB designs.
components.workingPrinciple: A miniature wound or multilayer coil stores energy in a magnetic field, providing inductance in a tiny surface-mount footprint.
components.currentRating: mA to several amps depending on size
disadvantages: Lower current handling than wound types
components.typicalUses: Compact switching power supplies on densely populated boards.
components.ttsSummary: Surface mount inductor used in compact power converters.

components.name: Bridge Rectifier
components.description: A four-diode arrangement that converts AC voltage into pulsating DC voltage, available as a single packaged module.
components.workingPrinciple: Four diodes arranged in a bridge configuration conduct in pairs during each half-cycle of AC, always directing current in the same direction through the load.
disadvantages: Voltage drop of ~1.2-1.4V across the bridge
components.typicalUses: Converting mains AC to DC in linear and switching power supplies.
components.ttsSummary: Bridge rectifier. Converts AC to DC using four diodes.

components.name: Zener Diode
components.description: A diode designed to conduct in reverse breakdown at a precise, stable voltage, used for voltage regulation.
components.workingPrinciple: When reverse biased beyond its breakdown (Zener) voltage, the diode conducts while holding the voltage across it nearly constant, providing voltage clamping/regulation.
components.typicalUses: Simple voltage references and clamping/protection circuits.
components.ttsSummary: Zener diode. Used for voltage regulation and reference.

components.name: Schottky Diode
components.description: A diode with a metal-semiconductor junction that switches faster and has a lower forward voltage drop than standard PN diodes.
components.workingPrinciple: A metal-semiconductor junction creates a low-barrier rectifying contact, enabling fast switching and lower forward voltage (~0.2-0.4V) than PN junction diodes.
components.typicalUses: High-frequency rectification and efficient power conversion.
components.ttsSummary: Schottky diode. Fast switching diode with low voltage drop.

components.name: LED (Light Emitting Diode)
components.description: A semiconductor diode that emits visible, infrared, or ultraviolet light when forward biased.
components.workingPrinciple: Forward current injects electrons and holes into a PN junction; their recombination releases energy as photons, with wavelength determined by the semiconductor's bandgap.
components.currentRating: Typically 5-20mA, high-power LEDs up to amps
components.typicalUses: Status indicators, lighting, and digital displays.
components.ttsSummary: LED, light emitting diode, used for indication and lighting.

components.name: Photodiode
components.description: A semiconductor diode that converts light into electrical current, used for light sensing.
components.workingPrinciple: Light photons absorbed near the PN junction generate electron-hole pairs, producing a photocurrent proportional to light intensity.
components.voltageRating: Reverse bias up to tens of volts
components.typicalUses: Light detection in sensors and optical receivers.
components.ttsSummary: Photodiode. Converts light into electrical current.

components.name: Laser Diode
components.description: A semiconductor diode that emits coherent laser light when driven above a threshold current.
components.workingPrinciple: Above threshold current, stimulated emission in the PN junction's active region produces coherent, monochromatic light amplified within an optical cavity.
components.typicalUses: Optical drives, barcode scanners, fiber communication.
components.ttsSummary: Laser diode. Emits a focused coherent light beam.

components.name: TVS Diode
components.description: A protection diode designed to clamp transient voltage spikes and protect sensitive circuits.
components.workingPrinciple: Similar to a Zener diode but optimized for very fast response, the TVS diode clamps to a defined voltage when a transient spike occurs, diverting surge current away from sensitive components.
components.currentRating: Peak pulse current rated in amps
components.typicalUses: Protecting I/O lines and power rails from voltage transients.
components.ttsSummary: TVS diode used to protect circuits from voltage spikes.

components.name: NPN Transistor
components.description: A bipolar junction transistor with N-type, P-type, N-type semiconductor layers, commonly used for switching and amplification.
components.workingPrinciple: A small base current controls a much larger collector-emitter current; electrons flow from emitter to collector when the base-emitter junction is forward biased.
disadvantages: Lower switching speed than MOSFET for power use
components.typicalUses: Signal amplification and low-to-medium power switching.
components.ttsSummary: NPN transistor used for switching and amplification.

components.name: PNP Transistor
components.description: A bipolar junction transistor with P-type, N-type, P-type semiconductor layers, conducting when the base is pulled low relative to the emitter.
components.workingPrinciple: Holes flow from emitter to collector when the base-emitter junction is forward biased with the base at a lower potential than the emitter; a small base current controls a larger emitter-collector current.
disadvantages: Generally lower gain than equivalent NPN
components.typicalUses: High-side load switching and complementary amplifier stages.
components.ttsSummary: PNP transistor used for high side switching.

components.name: MOSFET
components.description: A voltage-controlled transistor that uses an insulated gate to control current flow between drain and source, ideal for efficient switching.
components.workingPrinciple: Voltage applied to the gate creates an electric field that forms a conductive channel between drain and source through capacitive coupling, with negligible gate current at DC.
components.currentRating: Up to hundreds of amps for power types
disadvantages: Gate drive circuit needed for high power
components.typicalUses: Efficient power switching in converters, motor and LED drivers.
components.ttsSummary: MOSFET. A voltage controlled transistor for efficient switching.

components.name: IGBT
components.description: A hybrid transistor combining MOSFET gate control with BJT-like conduction, used in high-power, high-voltage switching applications.
components.workingPrinciple: An insulated MOSFET-style gate controls a bipolar conduction path, giving the easy gate drive of a MOSFET with the high-voltage, high-current handling of a BJT.
components.typicalUses: High-power motor drives and grid-level power conversion.
components.ttsSummary: IGBT used in high power motor drives and inverters.

components.name: JFET
components.description: A field-effect transistor that uses a reverse-biased PN junction to control channel conductivity, normally on when ungated.
components.workingPrinciple: A reverse-biased gate-channel junction creates a depletion region; increasing reverse gate voltage narrows the channel, reducing drain-source current.
components.typicalUses: Low-noise audio and instrumentation front ends.
components.ttsSummary: JFET. A low noise field effect transistor.

components.name: SCR (Thyristor)
components.description: A 4-layer semiconductor device that latches on when triggered and conducts until current drops to zero, used in power control.
components.workingPrinciple: A small gate current triggers regenerative feedback between the device's PNPN layers, latching it into full conduction until the anode current falls below a holding threshold.
disadvantages: Cannot be turned off via gate alone (needs current zero-crossing)
components.typicalUses: AC power switching and phase-controlled dimming.
components.ttsSummary: SCR thyristor used for AC power control.

components.name: TRIAC
components.description: A bidirectional thyristor that can conduct current in either direction once triggered, widely used for AC switching.
components.workingPrinciple: Two SCR structures are combined back-to-back, allowing the device to latch and conduct in both directions of an AC waveform when triggered by a gate pulse.
components.typicalUses: Household AC dimmers and AC motor speed controllers.
components.ttsSummary: TRIAC. A bidirectional switch used for AC dimming.

components.name: UJT (Unijunction Transistor)
components.description: A 3-terminal device with a single PN junction, primarily used to generate timing/triggering pulses for SCR and TRIAC circuits.
components.workingPrinciple: When emitter voltage exceeds a peak point determined by the intrinsic standoff ratio, the emitter-base1 junction switches into a low-resistance negative-resistance region, producing a pulse.
disadvantages: Largely replaced by IC timers like the 555
components.typicalUses: Generating trigger pulses for thyristor-based power control.
components.ttsSummary: UJT used to generate timing trigger pulses.

components.name: 555 Timer IC
components.description: A highly popular IC used to generate precise time delays or oscillations, configurable in astable, monostable, or bistable modes.
components.workingPrinciple: Two internal comparators monitor a capacitor charging/discharging through external resistors, toggling an internal flip-flop to produce timed output pulses.
components.pinConfiguration: 8-pin DIP: GND, TRIG, OUT, RESET, CTRL, THRES, VCC, DISCH
components.typicalUses: Astable/monostable timing circuits in hobby and industrial electronics.
components.ttsSummary: 555 timer IC used for generating timing and oscillation.

components.name: 741 Op-Amp
components.description: A classic general-purpose operational amplifier IC used for amplification, filtering, and signal conditioning.
components.workingPrinciple: A differential input stage amplifies the voltage difference between its two inputs, with internal compensation providing stable, high open-loop gain for analog signal processing.
components.pinConfiguration: 8-pin DIP: offset null, IN-, IN+, V-, NC, V+, OUT, offset null
disadvantages: Limited bandwidth and slew rate by modern standards
components.typicalUses: General-purpose analog amplification and filtering.
components.ttsSummary: 741 operational amplifier used for signal amplification.

components.name: LM358 Dual Op-Amp
components.description: A dual operational amplifier IC designed to operate from a single supply over a wide voltage range.
components.workingPrinciple: Two independent op-amps share the package, each amplifying the voltage difference between their inputs, optimized for single-supply operation down to low voltages.
components.pinConfiguration: 8-pin DIP: OUT1, IN1-, IN1+, GND, IN2+, IN2-, OUT2, VCC
disadvantages: Crossover distortion at low signal levels
components.typicalUses: Battery-powered sensor and signal-conditioning circuits.
components.ttsSummary: LM358 dual op amp used in sensor circuits.

components.name: LM324 Quad Op-Amp
components.description: A quad operational amplifier IC, packing four independent op-amps for single-supply analog circuits.
components.workingPrinciple: Four independent op-amp stages, each amplifying the difference between its two inputs, share a single package and power supply for compact multi-channel analog signal processing.
disadvantages: Crossover distortion at low signal levels
components.typicalUses: Compact multi-channel analog signal processing.
components.ttsSummary: LM324 quad op amp with four amplifiers in one chip.

components.name: 7805 Voltage Regulator
components.description: A linear voltage regulator IC that outputs a fixed regulated 5V from a higher unregulated input voltage.
components.workingPrinciple: An internal reference and feedback loop drive a pass transistor to maintain a constant 5V output, dissipating the voltage difference as heat.
components.powerRating: Dissipation dependent on Vin-Vout and current
components.typicalUses: Powering 5V logic circuits from a higher DC voltage.
components.ttsSummary: 7805 voltage regulator. Outputs a fixed five volts.

components.name: 7812 Voltage Regulator
components.description: A linear voltage regulator IC that outputs a fixed regulated 12V from a higher unregulated input voltage.
components.workingPrinciple: An internal reference and feedback loop drive a pass transistor to maintain a constant 12V output, dissipating the voltage difference as heat.
components.powerRating: Dissipation dependent on Vin-Vout and current
components.typicalUses: Powering 12V relay, fan, and analog circuits.
components.ttsSummary: 7812 voltage regulator. Outputs a fixed twelve volts.

components.name: ATmega328 Microcontroller
components.description: An 8-bit AVR microcontroller widely used in Arduino Uno and Nano boards, featuring flash memory, SRAM, and many peripherals.
components.workingPrinciple: A RISC-based CPU core executes instructions from flash memory, interfacing with GPIO, timers, ADC, and serial peripherals to control external circuits.
components.pinConfiguration: 28-pin DIP/TQFP: digital I/O, analog inputs, power, oscillator
advantages: Easy to program via Arduino IDE
disadvantages: Limited processing power and memory vs. modern MCUs
components.typicalUses: Hobbyist and educational embedded projects via Arduino.
components.ttsSummary: ATmega328 microcontroller, the chip used in Arduino Uno.

components.name: ESP32 Module
components.description: A powerful dual-core microcontroller module with integrated WiFi and Bluetooth, popular for IoT projects.
components.workingPrinciple: A dual-core Tensilica processor runs application code while integrated WiFi/Bluetooth radios handle wireless communication, with rich peripherals (ADC, PWM, I2C, SPI) for sensor/actuator interfacing.
components.pinConfiguration: 30/38-pin module: GPIO, ADC, UART, I2C, SPI, power
disadvantages: 3.3V logic only (not 5V tolerant on most pins)
components.typicalUses: WiFi/Bluetooth connected IoT and home automation projects.
components.ttsSummary: ESP32 module with built in WiFi and Bluetooth.

components.name: ESP8266 Module
components.description: A low-cost WiFi-enabled microcontroller module widely used in early IoT and home automation projects.
components.workingPrinciple: A single-core processor manages application logic while an integrated WiFi radio provides wireless network connectivity, communicating via GPIO/UART/SPI/I2C with external sensors.
components.pinConfiguration: Module pins: GPIO, UART, ADC, power
disadvantages: Single core, less powerful than ESP32
components.typicalUses: Budget WiFi-connected IoT sensor and control projects.
components.ttsSummary: ESP8266 module, a low cost WiFi enabled microcontroller.

components.name: Arduino Uno
components.description: A beginner-friendly development board built around the ATmega328P microcontroller, the most widely used Arduino board.
components.workingPrinciple: The onboard ATmega328P executes user-uploaded code, with onboard voltage regulation, USB-to-serial, and labeled headers exposing GPIO, analog, and power pins for easy prototyping.
components.pinConfiguration: 14 digital I/O, 6 analog inputs, power headers
components.voltageRating: Operating 5V, input via USB or 7-12V jack
components.typicalUses: Learning embedded electronics and rapid prototyping.
components.ttsSummary: Arduino Uno development board.

components.name: Arduino Nano
components.description: A compact version of the Arduino Uno, using the same ATmega328P microcontroller in a smaller breadboard-friendly form factor.
components.workingPrinciple: Identical core operation to the Arduino Uno; the ATmega328P runs user code while onboard regulation and USB-to-serial circuitry handle power and programming.
disadvantages: No onboard barrel power jack on most versions
components.typicalUses: Compact embedded projects requiring breadboard mounting.
components.ttsSummary: Arduino Nano, a compact development board.

components.name: NodeMCU Board
components.description: A development board built around the ESP8266, providing easy USB programming and WiFi connectivity for IoT projects.
components.workingPrinciple: An onboard ESP8266 module runs application firmware while a USB-to-serial chip allows direct programming from a computer, exposing GPIO pins broken out for easy prototyping.
components.typicalUses: Rapid IoT prototyping with built-in WiFi.
components.ttsSummary: NodeMCU board built around the ESP8266 WiFi chip.

components.name: Raspberry Pi Pico
components.description: A low-cost microcontroller board built around the dual-core RP2040 chip, designed by Raspberry Pi.
components.workingPrinciple: A dual-core ARM Cortex-M0+ processor (RP2040) executes user firmware, with flexible programmable I/O (PIO) blocks enabling custom peripheral interfacing beyond standard GPIO.
disadvantages: No onboard WiFi (unless Pico W variant)
components.typicalUses: Custom protocol and timing-critical embedded applications.
components.ttsSummary: Raspberry Pi Pico, a low cost dual core microcontroller board.

components.name: Crystal Oscillator
components.description: A component that uses the mechanical resonance of a vibrating quartz crystal to generate a precise, stable clock frequency.
components.workingPrinciple: The piezoelectric quartz crystal resonates mechanically at a precise frequency when electrically excited, and this resonance is used to stabilize an oscillator circuit's output frequency.
components.typicalUses: Providing stable clock signals to microcontrollers and ICs.
components.ttsSummary: Crystal oscillator used to generate a stable clock frequency.

components.name: Relay
components.description: An electromagnetically operated switch that allows a low-power control signal to switch a higher-power circuit.
components.workingPrinciple: Current through an electromagnetic coil generates a magnetic field that pulls an armature, mechanically closing or opening a separate set of switch contacts.
applications: Isolation between control and power circuits
advantages: Can switch high power with low control power
components.typicalUses: Switching mains-powered loads from microcontroller logic.
components.ttsSummary: Relay. An electromagnetic switch used to control high power loads.

components.name: Buzzer
components.description: An audio signaling device that produces sound, available in piezoelectric and electromagnetic types.
components.workingPrinciple: A piezo buzzer vibrates a piezoelectric diaphragm when driven by an oscillating voltage, while an electromagnetic buzzer vibrates a diaphragm using a coil and magnet, both producing audible sound.
components.typicalUses: Alarm and notification sounds in embedded devices.
components.ttsSummary: Buzzer used for audible alerts and notifications.

components.name: Switch
components.description: A mechanical device used to manually make or break an electrical circuit connection.
components.workingPrinciple: Moving a mechanical actuator (toggle, slide, or rocker) physically connects or disconnects internal metal contacts, completing or breaking the circuit path.
components.pinConfiguration: 2 to 6 terminals depending on type (SPST to DPDT)
components.voltageRating: Up to 250VAC for many panel switches
components.typicalUses: Manual on/off and mode selection in devices.
components.ttsSummary: Switch used to manually control circuit connections.

components.name: Push Button
components.description: A momentary switch that closes a circuit only while pressed, commonly used for user input.
components.workingPrinciple: Pressing the button physically deforms a metal dome or spring contact, briefly bridging two internal contacts to complete the circuit, releasing reopens it.
components.typicalUses: User interface buttons and reset inputs on embedded boards.
components.ttsSummary: Push button used for momentary user input.

components.name: LDR (Light Dependent Resistor)
components.description: A resistor whose resistance changes based on the amount of light falling on it, commonly used for light sensing.
components.workingPrinciple: Light photons striking the cadmium sulfide (CdS) material free charge carriers, lowering the material's resistance as light intensity increases.
components.typicalUses: Automatic lighting control and basic light-level detection.
components.ttsSummary: LDR, a light dependent resistor used for light sensing.

components.name: Thermistor
components.description: A temperature-sensitive resistor whose resistance changes predictably with temperature, used in temperature sensing and compensation.
components.workingPrinciple: NTC thermistors decrease resistance as temperature rises (more thermally excited charge carriers), while PTC thermistors increase resistance with temperature, both following a characteristic resistance-temperature curve.
components.typicalUses: Temperature measurement and protection circuits.
components.ttsSummary: Thermistor used for sensing temperature.

components.name: IR Sensor
components.description: A sensor module that detects infrared light, typically used to sense proximity, obstacles, or remote control signals.
components.workingPrinciple: An IR LED emits infrared light which reflects off nearby objects and is detected by an IR photodiode/phototransistor; the received signal strength indicates presence or distance.
disadvantages: Affected by ambient light and reflective surface color
components.typicalUses: Robot obstacle avoidance and proximity detection.
components.ttsSummary: Infrared sensor used for obstacle and proximity detection.

components.name: Ultrasonic Sensor (HC-SR04)
components.description: A distance-measuring sensor module that uses ultrasonic sound waves to calculate distance to an object.
components.workingPrinciple: A transmitter emits an ultrasonic pulse that reflects off an object; the receiver detects the echo, and distance is calculated from the time delay using the speed of sound.
disadvantages: Affected by soft/angled surfaces that absorb/scatter sound
components.typicalUses: Robotics distance sensing and parking assist systems.
components.ttsSummary: Ultrasonic sensor used to measure distance using sound waves.

components.name: Hall Effect Sensor
components.description: A sensor that detects the presence and strength of a magnetic field, commonly used for position and speed sensing.
components.workingPrinciple: A magnetic field deflects moving charge carriers in a thin semiconductor strip, generating a small transverse voltage (the Hall voltage) proportional to field strength.
disadvantages: Requires a magnet for target detection
components.typicalUses: Contactless speed and position sensing in motors and wheels.
components.ttsSummary: Hall effect sensor used to detect magnetic fields.

components.name: Temperature Sensor (LM35/DS18B20)
components.description: A sensor IC that measures ambient or contact temperature and outputs an analog voltage or digital reading.
components.workingPrinciple: Analog sensors like the LM35 output a voltage linearly proportional to temperature, while digital sensors like the DS18B20 use an internal temperature-dependent oscillator and ADC to report temperature over a digital bus.
disadvantages: Analog types susceptible to noise over long wires
components.typicalUses: Temperature monitoring in embedded and IoT projects.
components.ttsSummary: Temperature sensor used for monitoring temperature.

components.name: Humidity Sensor (DHT11/DHT22)
components.description: A sensor module that measures relative humidity, often combined with temperature sensing in one package.
components.workingPrinciple: A capacitive humidity sensing element changes capacitance with moisture absorption, which is measured and converted to a digital humidity reading by an internal microcontroller.
advantages: Combined temperature and humidity in one module
components.typicalUses: Environmental monitoring in IoT and weather projects.
components.ttsSummary: Humidity sensor used for measuring relative humidity.

components.name: Accelerometer
components.description: A sensor that measures acceleration forces, used to detect motion, tilt, and vibration.
components.workingPrinciple: A microscopic mass on a spring-like MEMS structure shifts under acceleration, changing capacitance between sensing elements, which is converted into a proportional electrical signal.
components.pinConfiguration: I2C/SPI module: VCC, GND, SDA, SCL
disadvantages: Susceptible to drift and noise, needs filtering
components.typicalUses: Motion sensing in drones, wearables, and robotics.
components.ttsSummary: Accelerometer used to measure motion and tilt.

components.name: Gyroscope
components.description: A sensor that measures angular velocity (rate of rotation), often paired with an accelerometer for full motion sensing.
components.workingPrinciple: A MEMS vibrating structure experiences a Coriolis force proportional to angular velocity when rotated, which is detected capacitively and converted to an electrical rotation-rate signal.
components.pinConfiguration: I2C/SPI module: VCC, GND, SDA, SCL
disadvantages: Drift over time without sensor fusion
components.typicalUses: Orientation and stabilization in drones and robotics.
components.ttsSummary: Gyroscope used to measure rotation rate.

components.name: GPS Module
components.description: A module that receives satellite signals to determine geographic location, altitude, and time.
components.workingPrinciple: The module receives timed signals from multiple GPS satellites and calculates its position via trilateration based on signal travel time differences.
components.pinConfiguration: UART module: VCC, GND, TX, RX
components.typicalUses: Vehicle and asset tracking, navigation projects.
components.ttsSummary: GPS module used for satellite based positioning.

components.name: Bluetooth Module (HC-05)
components.description: A wireless communication module that enables short-range Bluetooth connectivity between devices.
components.workingPrinciple: The module implements the Bluetooth protocol stack, modulating data over the 2.4GHz ISM band to establish a wireless serial link with a paired device.
components.pinConfiguration: UART module: VCC, GND, TX, RX, optional EN/STATE
components.typicalUses: Wireless control of robots and devices via smartphone apps.
components.ttsSummary: Bluetooth module used for short range wireless communication.

components.name: WiFi Module
components.description: A module providing wireless network connectivity, enabling devices to connect to WiFi networks for data communication.
components.workingPrinciple: The module implements the 802.11 WiFi protocol stack, modulating data over 2.4GHz/5GHz bands to communicate with a wireless access point or router.
components.typicalUses: Connecting embedded devices to the internet for IoT applications.
components.ttsSummary: WiFi module providing wireless internet connectivity.

components.name: RF Module (433MHz)
components.description: A simple radio frequency transmitter/receiver pair used for basic wireless data transmission over short to medium range.
components.workingPrinciple: The transmitter module modulates a radio carrier (typically 433MHz) with the input data using ASK/OOK modulation, while the receiver demodulates the signal to recover the data.
components.pinConfiguration: Transmitter: VCC, GND, DATA, ANT; Receiver: VCC, GND, DATA×2, ANT
components.typicalUses: Simple remote control and one-way sensor data links.
components.ttsSummary: RF module used for simple wireless remote control links.

components.name: OLED Display
components.description: A thin, low-power display using organic light-emitting diodes for each pixel, offering high contrast and wide viewing angles.
components.workingPrinciple: Each pixel contains an organic compound that emits light directly when current flows through it, eliminating the need for a backlight and allowing true blacks.
components.pinConfiguration: I2C/SPI module: VCC, GND, SDA/SCL or SPI lines
disadvantages: Smaller sizes common, limited color (often monochrome)
components.typicalUses: Compact status displays in embedded and wearable projects.
components.ttsSummary: OLED display used for compact embedded user interfaces.

components.name: LCD Display (16x2)
components.description: A character display using liquid crystal technology, commonly used for showing text in embedded systems.
components.workingPrinciple: Liquid crystal molecules between polarizing filters twist under an applied voltage, blocking or passing backlight to form visible characters in a dot-matrix grid.
components.typicalUses: Displaying status text and menus in embedded systems.
components.ttsSummary: LCD display used to show text status messages.

components.name: Seven Segment Display
components.description: A display made of seven LED segments arranged to show numeric digits 0-9 and some letters.
components.workingPrinciple: Each of the seven LED segments is individually lit by driving its corresponding pin, with combinations of lit segments forming the visual shape of digits.
disadvantages: Limited to digits and few characters
components.typicalUses: Numeric display in clocks, counters, and meters.
components.ttsSummary: Seven segment display used to show numeric digits.

components.name: Stepper Motor
components.description: A motor that moves in discrete, fixed-angle steps, allowing precise control of position without feedback.
components.workingPrinciple: Sequentially energizing coil pairs creates rotating magnetic fields that pull the rotor's teeth into alignment one fixed step at a time, allowing precise open-loop position control.
disadvantages: Can lose steps under excessive load
Less efficient than servo at speed
components.typicalUses: Precision positioning in 3D printers and CNC machines.
components.ttsSummary: Stepper motor used for precise positional control.

components.name: Servo Motor
components.description: A motor with built-in position feedback control, commonly used for precise angular positioning in robotics.
components.workingPrinciple: An internal potentiometer provides position feedback to a control circuit, which drives a small DC motor through gearing until the shaft reaches the angle commanded by the input PWM signal.
disadvantages: Limited rotation range (typically 180°) for standard servos
components.typicalUses: Angular positioning in robotics and RC models.
components.ttsSummary: Servo motor used for precise angular positioning.

components.name: DC Motor
components.description: An electric motor that converts direct current electrical energy into continuous rotational mechanical motion.
components.workingPrinciple: Current through motor windings creates a magnetic field that interacts with permanent magnets, producing torque that rotates the shaft, with brushes mechanically switching current direction to sustain rotation.
components.currentRating: 100mA to several amps depending on size
components.powerRating: Fractional watt to hundreds of watts
components.typicalUses: Driving wheels, fans, and simple mechanical actuators.
components.ttsSummary: DC motor used for continuous rotational motion.

components.name: BLDC Motor
components.description: A brushless electric motor that uses electronic commutation instead of mechanical brushes, offering higher efficiency and longevity.
components.workingPrinciple: An electronic speed controller sequentially energizes stator windings based on rotor position (often sensed via Hall sensors), creating a rotating magnetic field that drives the permanent-magnet rotor without mechanical brushes.
components.voltageRating: Varies widely, 7V to 50V+ common for drones
components.currentRating: Several amps to tens of amps
disadvantages: Requires an electronic speed controller (ESC) to operate
components.typicalUses: High-efficiency propulsion in drones and electric vehicles.
components.ttsSummary: Brushless DC motor used for drones and electric vehicles.

components.name: Battery
components.description: An electrochemical energy storage device that provides DC power to electronic circuits.
components.workingPrinciple: Chemical reactions between the anode, cathode, and electrolyte release electrons, generating a flow of current through an external circuit while ions move internally to balance charge.
components.voltageRating: 1.2V (NiMH) to 3.7V (Li-ion) per cell, higher in packs
components.currentRating: Depends on capacity (mAh) and discharge rate
components.packageType: Cylindrical cell, pouch cell, coin cell
components.typicalUses: Powering portable and battery-backed electronic devices.
components.ttsSummary: Battery used to store and supply electrical energy.

components.name: Fuse
components.description: A protective device that interrupts current flow by melting a thin wire element when current exceeds a safe threshold.
components.workingPrinciple: Excessive current heats a thin metal element past its melting point, breaking the circuit and preventing damage to downstream components or wiring.
components.currentRating: Rated trip current from mA to tens of amps
components.typicalUses: Protecting circuits and equipment from overcurrent damage.
components.ttsSummary: Fuse used to protect circuits from excessive current.

components.name: USB Connector
components.description: A standardized connector used for power delivery and data transfer between electronic devices.
components.workingPrinciple: Physical pins/contacts in the connector carry power and differential data signal lines, following the USB protocol standard for plug-and-play device communication.
disadvantages: Mechanical wear on connector after many cycles
components.typicalUses: Charging and data connectivity for electronic devices.
components.ttsSummary: USB connector used for power and data connections.

components.name: RJ45 Connector
components.description: A standardized 8-pin connector used for Ethernet networking cables.
components.workingPrinciple: Eight metal contacts in the connector interface with corresponding twisted-pair conductors in the cable, carrying differential Ethernet signal pairs to the network interface.
components.voltageRating: Low voltage signal level (PoE variants up to 57V)
components.typicalUses: Wired Ethernet network connections.
components.ttsSummary: RJ45 connector used for wired ethernet networking.

components.name: Terminal Block
components.description: A modular connector that uses screws or clamps to securely connect wires to a circuit without soldering.
components.workingPrinciple: A screw or spring-clamp mechanism presses a bare wire against a metal contact, providing a secure mechanical and electrical connection that can be easily disconnected and reconnected.
components.pinConfiguration: Multiple poles, screw or spring clamp
components.voltageRating: Up to 300V or higher depending on rating
components.currentRating: Up to tens of amps depending on size
components.typicalUses: Connecting field wiring in industrial and panel-mounted equipment.
components.ttsSummary: Terminal block used for solderless wire connections.

components.name: Breadboard
components.description: A reusable prototyping board with a grid of interconnected holes, allowing components to be connected without soldering.
components.workingPrinciple: Internal metal spring clips connect rows/columns of holes together, allowing component leads inserted into connected holes to be electrically joined without soldering.
components.typicalUses: Quick circuit prototyping and educational demonstrations.
components.ttsSummary: Breadboard used for solderless circuit prototyping.

components.name: PCB (Printed Circuit Board)
components.description: A board with conductive copper traces etched onto an insulating substrate, used to mechanically support and electrically connect components.
components.workingPrinciple: Etched copper traces on the board's surface (or internal layers) form the circuit's electrical connections, replacing point-to-point wiring with a fixed, repeatable layout.
components.currentRating: Design dependent (trace width determines max current)
disadvantages: Requires design and fabrication lead time
components.typicalUses: Final assembled product circuits and mass-produced electronics.
components.ttsSummary: Printed circuit board used to mount and connect components.

components.name: Voltage Regulator (General)
components.description: A device that maintains a constant output voltage despite variations in input voltage or load current.
components.workingPrinciple: A feedback control loop continuously compares output voltage to a reference and adjusts a pass element (linear) or switching duty cycle (switching) to maintain a stable, regulated output.
components.currentRating: 100mA to several amps depending on part
disadvantages: Linear types waste energy as heat
components.typicalUses: Providing stable regulated voltage rails in circuits.
components.ttsSummary: Voltage regulator used to maintain a stable output voltage.

components.name: Heat Sink
components.description: A passive component that dissipates heat away from electronic components to prevent overheating.
components.workingPrinciple: Increased surface area (often with fins) improves convective and radiative heat transfer from the attached component to the surrounding air, lowering the component's operating temperature.
components.typicalUses: Thermal management for power semiconductors and regulators.
components.ttsSummary: Heat sink used to dissipate heat from components.

components.name: Crystal
components.description: A small piezoelectric resonator (without integrated oscillator circuit) used in conjunction with external circuitry to generate a precise frequency.
components.workingPrinciple: The crystal mechanically resonates at a specific frequency determined by its physical cut and dimensions when electrically stimulated, providing a stable frequency reference for an external oscillator circuit.
disadvantages: Needs external oscillator circuitry to function
components.typicalUses: Providing a frequency reference for RTC and MCU clocks.
components.ttsSummary: Crystal resonator used to provide a stable frequency reference.

components.name: Optocoupler
components.description: A device that transfers electrical signals between two isolated circuits using light, providing electrical isolation.
components.workingPrinciple: An internal LED converts the input electrical signal into light, which is detected by a phototransistor or photodiode on the isolated output side, transferring the signal without a direct electrical connection.
components.pinConfiguration: 4 to 6-pin DIP: input LED pins, output detector pins
components.voltageRating: Isolation voltage up to several kV
components.currentRating: Input LED ~10-20mA, output transistor mA range
disadvantages: Aging reduces LED output over time
components.typicalUses: Isolating logic circuits from high voltage or noisy circuits.
components.ttsSummary: Optocoupler used to isolate signals using light.

components.name: Current Sensor
components.description: A sensor that measures the current flowing through a circuit, often using Hall-effect or shunt-resistor techniques.
components.workingPrinciple: Hall-effect current sensors measure the magnetic field generated by current flow without breaking the circuit, while shunt-based sensors measure the voltage drop across a precision low-value resistor proportional to current.
disadvantages: Accuracy can be affected by temperature drift
components.typicalUses: Monitoring current draw in battery and motor systems.
components.ttsSummary: Current sensor used to measure circuit current.

components.name: Voltage Sensor
components.description: A module that scales down higher voltages to a safe level that can be read by a microcontroller's analog input.
components.workingPrinciple: A precision resistive voltage divider scales the input voltage down to a safe range, allowing a microcontroller's ADC to measure and calculate the original voltage.
components.typicalUses: Monitoring battery and supply voltages in embedded systems.
components.ttsSummary: Voltage sensor used to measure higher voltages safely.

components.name: Load Cell
components.description: A transducer that converts mechanical force (weight) into an electrical signal, used in weighing scales.
components.workingPrinciple: Strain gauges bonded to a metal body change resistance slightly when the body deforms under load, and this resistance change is measured via a Wheatstone bridge to determine applied force.
disadvantages: Requires precision amplifier (e.g., HX711) for small signals
components.typicalUses: Weight measurement in digital scales and industrial systems.
components.ttsSummary: Load cell used to measure weight and force.

components.name: RTC Module (DS3231)
components.description: A module that keeps accurate time and date even when the main system is powered off, using a backup battery.
components.workingPrinciple: An internal crystal-controlled oscillator counts time continuously, maintained by a backup coin-cell battery during power loss, with the current time/date readable over I2C.
components.pinConfiguration: I2C module: VCC, GND, SDA, SCL
components.packageType: Module board with coin cell holder
disadvantages: Requires backup battery for power-loss timekeeping
components.typicalUses: Timestamping data logs and scheduling tasks in embedded projects.
components.ttsSummary: RTC module used to keep accurate time even when powered off.

components.name: Micro SD Module
components.description: A module that allows a microcontroller to read and write data to a micro SD card for storage.
components.workingPrinciple: The module provides level-shifted SPI communication between the microcontroller and the SD card's native SPI/SDIO interface, enabling file read/write operations.
components.pinConfiguration: SPI module: VCC, GND, MISO, MOSI, SCK, CS
disadvantages: File system overhead requires library support
components.typicalUses: Data logging and file storage in embedded systems.
components.ttsSummary: Micro SD module used for data storage in embedded systems.

components.name: Camera Module
components.description: A small camera sensor module that captures images or video for embedded vision applications.
components.workingPrinciple: A CMOS or CCD image sensor converts incoming light into electrical signals pixel by pixel, which are processed into a digital image stream output over a parallel or serial interface.
components.pinConfiguration: Parallel/MIPI/SPI interface: data, clock, power lines
advantages: Enables vision capability in embedded projects
disadvantages: Requires significant processing power for image handling
components.typicalUses: Embedded vision and image capture projects.
components.ttsSummary: Camera module used to capture images in embedded systems.
